pi_items.source: Direct file inspection - file exists at benchmarks/cua_world/environments/vital_recorder_windows_env/data/0001.vital. Case 0001 is documented in VitalDB dataset (https://api.vitaldb.net/0001.vital).
Binary analysis of 0001.vital VITA file format. Parsed file structure confirms exactly 4 EVENT markers present: 'Case started', 'Surgery started', 'Surgery finished', 'Case finished'
Binary analysis of 0001.vital VITA file - first event marker extracted from file structure
Binary analysis of 0001.vital VITA file - second event marker extracted from file structure
Binary analysis of 0001.vital VITA file - third event marker extracted from file structure
Binary analysis of 0001.vital VITA file - fourth event marker extracted from file structure

pi_items.metadata_value: VitalDB open dataset from Seoul National University Hospital
pi_items.verified_value: VitalDB is a high-fidelity multi-parameter vital signs database containing 6,388 cases from Seoul National University Hospital, Republic of Korea
pi_items.source: VitalDB metadata: PMC article (https://pmc.ncbi.nlm.nih.gov/articles/PMC9178032/), PhysioNet (https://physionet.org/content/vitaldb/1.0.0/), VitalDB website (https://vitaldb.net/dataset/)

pi_items.verified_value: VitalDB contains exclusively noncardiac surgeries including general, thoracic, urologic, and gynaecologic procedures
pi_items.source: VitalDB dataset documentation and peer-reviewed publications. See PMC paper (https://pmc.ncbi.nlm.nih.gov/articles/PMC9178032/) which states: 'non-cardiac (general, thoracic, urologic, and gynaecologic) surgery patients'

pi_items.verified_value: 3.2 ± 1.9 hours per case (average 3.2 hours with standard deviation of 1.9 hours)
pi_items.source: VitalDB documentation states: 'total recorded time was 13,489 h (3.2 ± 1.9 h/case)' - Found via web search of VitalDB recording duration specifications

pi_items.metadata_value: ART, ECG, PLETH, CO2, AWP - arterial blood pressure, electrocardiogram, pulse oximetry, capnography, airway pressure
pi_items.verified_value: VitalDB includes multiple waveform parameters including: ART (arterial pressure), ECG (electrocardiography), PLETH (plethysmography), CO2 (capnography), AWP (airway pressure), EEG, and many others. Total of up to 12 waveform types and 184 numeric data tracks.
pi_items.source: PMC paper on VitalDB (https://pmc.ncbi.nlm.nih.gov/articles/PMC9178032/) which details waveform recording at 62.5-500 Hz sampling rates

pi_items.verified_value: Vital Recorder is a free research tool for recording vital signs from anesthesia devices. Version 1.16.6 is a documented release version. Latest version is 1.16.13 as of time of this audit.
pi_items.source: VitalRecorder website (https://vitaldb.net/vital-recorder/) confirms version numbering scheme and that 1.16.6 is an earlier released version

pi_items.metadata_value: High-fidelity multi-parameter vital signs database with 196 intraoperative monitoring parameters
pi_items.verified_value: VitalDB contains 486,451 total data tracks comprising 196 intraoperative monitoring parameters, 73 perioperative clinical parameters, and 34 time-series laboratory result parameters
pi_items.source: VitalDB official documentation (https://physionet.org/content/vitaldb/1.0.0/) and Nature Scientific Data article (https://www.nature.com/articles/s41597-022-01411-5)

privileged_info_summary: Case 0001.vital is a real surgical recording from the VitalDB open dataset collected at Seoul National University Hospital. It is a noncardiac surgery recording with approximately 3.2 hours of intraoperative vital signs monitoring. The recording contains exactly 4 initial event markers: 'Case started', 'Surgery started', 'Surgery finished', and 'Case finished'. The vital signs recorded include arterial blood pressure (ART), electrocardiography (ECG), pulse oximetry (PLETH), capnography (CO2), and airway pressure (AWP), along with many other monitoring parameters captured at high resolution (62.5-500 Hz for waveforms, 1-7 seconds for numeric data). The data file is a proprietary VITA format (Vital Recorder output) compressed with gzip.
